raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Both students with disabilities and their non-disabled peers benefit from inclusion in general education.",
    "Disabled students who are included show a lower rate of dropout, suspension, failure in employment, and other risk factors.",
    "California had one of the lowest inclusion rates for students with disabilities in the country in the 2017-18 school year, at 56% compared to a national average of a little over 63%.",
    "Senate Bill 1113 aims to provide schools with resources and technical assistance to foster inclusive classroom environments.",
    "If passed, Senate Bill 1113 will identify barriers to inclusion and ensure the curriculum is universally beneficial for all students.",
    "Senate Bill 1113 will fund technical assistance providers and conduct a study to determine appropriate staffing numbers to achieve best practices for inclusion.",
    "Training in inclusive practices for new school administrators is a requirement of Senate Bill 1113.",
    "Allowing abled and disabled children to learn in a least restrictive environment is vital for student achievement.",
    "Educating parents, teachers, and administrators about the value of including students with disabilities alongside their peers promotes the development of healthy relationships and future success."
  ]
}
```
